metadata.null_method: Two nulls: (1) random 5-term seqs in EC range vs OEIS fps, (2) leave-one-out cross-transform

interpretation.finding_1_pi_e_degenerate: T01_div_pi and T02_div_e produce IDENTICAL fingerprints (m3=406, m5=225, OEIS=184). At small a_p values, round(a/pi) and round(a/e) differ by at most 1, which vanishes mod 3. No independent e-wormhole exists.
interpretation.finding_2_fingerprint_collapse: T12_scaled (500/500 m3 matches, 30 OEIS) and T16_ratio (423/450, 130 OEIS) suffer from fingerprint collapse: many EC curves map to the same few fingerprints. High match count with low OEIS coverage = degenerate, not genuine.

finding_3_mod5_discriminators.description: mod-5 fingerprints are much more discriminating (360/3125 = 11.5% coverage vs 165/243 = 67.9% for mod-3). Transforms with high mod-5 z-scores are the real signals.

finding_4_genuine_bridges.T19_abs: Taking |a_p| removes sign information, revealing that MANY OEIS sequences match unsigned EC trace patterns. 426 OEIS covered. This is a structural bridge: OEIS sequences are typically positive, and EC a_p are symmetric around 0.
finding_4_genuine_bridges.T18_mod_spf: Reducing a_p mod smallest-prime-factor-of-conductor creates bridges through the arithmetic of bad primes. 293 OEIS covered, z5=32.3.
finding_4_genuine_bridges.T06_mod_cond: a_p mod N reduces to conductor residues. 417 OEIS, z5=9.2. Conductor is the natural scale.
finding_4_genuine_bridges.T20_sign: Sign sequence (+1/-1/0) matches 389 OEIS sequences. Surprising coverage suggests many OEIS sequences share sign patterns with EC traces.

interpretation.finding_5_no_new_constant_wormhole: No transform involving a mathematical constant (sqrt(2), phi, ln(2)) produces matches significantly above identity at mod-5. The pi wormhole remains unique among constant-based transforms. sqrt(2) and phi are marginally above noise at mod-5 (z=2.9 and 3.7) but not above the identity baseline.
interpretation.finding_6_dead_zone_structure: The dead zone is not bridgeable by constant division alone. The transforms that gain most ground are STRUCTURAL (abs, mod-conductor, sign) not CONSTANT-based. The remaining 17% gap requires semantic matching, not arithmetic transforms.

gap_status.mod3_baseline: 88.4% coverage (442/500 OEIS covered by identity alone)
gap_status.combined_all_genuine: 100% at mod-3 (trivial), need mod-5 analysis for real gap
